name: Alto do Boi Loop
name_original: Ribas de Sil Vuelta - Reserva da Biosfera Ribeira Sacra
description: This difficult 35.8-mile (57.6 km) road cycling loop offers 5315 feet (1620 metres) of elevation gain in the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve
region_intro: Road cycling around Folgoso Do Courel offers diverse terrain within the UNESCO Global Geopark Courel Mountains. The region features dramatic mountainous landscapes, with peaks reaching over 1600 meters, and valleys carved by rivers like the Lor, Soldón, and Quiroga. Cyclists can navigate well-maintained roads through ancient chestnut groves, humid devesas, and past significant geological formations. This area provides a blend of challenging ascents and scenic routes along river systems. Best road cycling routes around Folgoso Do Courel The most popular road cycling route is Ribas de Sil Return - Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve, a 35.8 miles (57.6 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto do Cebreiro (1300 m) – Samos (Galicia, Spain) loop from A Pereiriña, a difficult 101.5 miles (163.4 km) path. This extensive route includes substantial climbing and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Alto do Cebreiro (1300 m) – O Cebreiro loop from O Poio, a 23.5 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Folgoso Do Courel is defined by dramatic mountainous terrain, river valleys, and ancient forests. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, with no easy routes available. The routes in Folgoso Do Courel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 7 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Folgoso Do Courel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Folgoso Do Courel listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level for road cycling in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Road cycling in Folgoso Do Courel is generally challenging, with routes primarily categorized as moderate or difficult. The region's dramatic mountainous terrain means you can expect significant elevation gains. There are no easy routes available.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: The road cycling routes in Folgoso Do Courel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning panoramic views and the challenging ascents through unique geological formations.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: The best time to visit Folgoso do Courel for road cycling is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery, such as the stunning colors of the Hayedo de Busmayor in autumn. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially warmer weather on exposed climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: While road cycling, you can encounter numerous natural features and landmarks. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lor River&lt;/a&gt;, which carves through valleys, and impressive waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Spring Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beiro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the UNESCO Global Geopark Courel Mountains, known for its unique geological heritage.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Folgoso Do Courel offers several challenging routes for experienced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943529140/ribas-de-sil-return-ribeira-sacra-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribas de Sil Return - Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 57.6 km route with significant elevation gain. Another extensive and difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Cebreiro (1300 m) – Samos (Galicia, Spain) loop from A Pereiriña&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 160 km with substantial climbing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: You can expect a diverse and dramatic terrain. Routes navigate through mountainous landscapes with peaks over 1600 meters, along river valleys carved by the Lor, Soldón, and Quiroga rivers, and through ancient chestnut groves and humid 'devesas'. The roads are generally well-maintained, offering a mix of challenging ascents and scenic stretches.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints accessible by road bike in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Statue at Alto de San Roque&lt;/a&gt; offers unique panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Another excellent spot is Mirador de Mostaz, providing exceptional views of the Lor River valley and distant mountain ranges.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38253307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Cebreiro (1300 m) – O Cebreiro loop from O Poio&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 37.9 km trail that takes you through mountainous terrain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31855901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from San Clodio-Quiroga&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 62.5 km route.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional villages or historical sites?
A: Yes, the area is rich in cultural heritage. Many routes will take you past traditional villages and historical sites. You might encounter ancient mills or even the Castle of Carbedo. The region is also known for its unique 'alvares,' traditional stone structures used to protect beehives, which can be seen along certain routes.

Q: Is there parking available near the start of road cycling routes in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Folgoso Do Courel is generally accessible. Many routes start from or pass through villages where parking might be available. It's advisable to check local information for parking options at your chosen starting point.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities in Folgoso Do Courel?
A: Folgoso Do Courel is an excellent destination for combining road cycling with other outdoor pursuits. The region offers some of the most impressive hiking routes, ranging from easy to difficult, often leading through dense forests and across mountain landscapes. You can also explore historic villages and natural monuments.
tips: Stone bridge over the Sil river. get ready to climb

On the way up from Quiroga, the mountain range of Courel welcomes you, surrounded by a protected forest landscape and incredible views. The rocky formation and the cliff front are of notable geological importance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Alto do Boi Loop?
faqs.answer: The Alto do Boi Loop is a challenging route, and for an average racebike rider, it typically takes around 3 hours and 34 minutes to complete. This duration can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Alto do Boi Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant distance of over 57 kilometers, and substantial elevation gain of more than 1600 meters, the Alto do Boi Loop is generally not recommended for beginners. It's best suited for experienced racebike riders looking for a demanding challenge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The Alto do Boi Loop is designed for racebikes, so you can expect mostly well-paved surfaces. However, with over 1600 meters of elevation gain, be prepared for numerous and often steep climbs. Some sections might be twisty or narrow, requiring careful navigation. The route is known for its challenging ascents, characteristic of mountain cycling in the region.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the Alto do Boi Loop?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. Keep an eye out for the historic Ponte Nova de San Clodio-Quiroga, a charming bridge. You'll also pass by the Campodola-Leixazós Geological Viewpoint, which offers stunning panoramic views of the landscape.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alto do Boi Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for the Alto do Boi Loop aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in the nearby towns of Folgoso do Courel or Quiroga. These towns serve as common starting points for routes in the region and offer amenities for cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle the Alto do Boi Loop is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for adverse weather conditions in the mountains.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling the Alto do Boi Loop?
faqs.answer: The Alto do Boi Loop is located within the Ribeira Sacra e Serras do Oribio e Courel Biosphere Reserve. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the reserve. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and stay on designated routes.

faqs.question: Is the Alto do Boi Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a racebike route primarily on public roads, the Alto do Boi Loop isn't typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the same way a hiking trail might be. Cycling with a dog requires specific equipment and training, and it's essential to ensure your dog's safety and comfort, especially on a demanding route with traffic. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are securely leashed or in a proper trailer, and always adhere to local regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Alto do Boi Loop incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the [CIMA LU01] O Boi * Quiroga, [CIMA LU01] O Boi * Folgoso do Courel, and the historic Camiño de Inverno. Smaller segments also overlap with [CIMA LU01] O Boi * Baldomir and LU-M-017019.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a racebike ride on the Alto do Boi Loop?
faqs.answer: For a challenging route like this, essential gear includes a well-maintained racebike, appropriate cycling apparel, a helmet, plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a fully charged phone. Given the elevation, consider layers of clothing, and always check the weather forecast before you set out.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is quite rural, the towns of Folgoso do Courel and Quiroga, which are common access points, offer cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's advisable to carry sufficient snacks and water for the duration of the loop, as options directly on the trail may be limited.
